stated.purpose: LIFE is the EU programme protecting the environment and climate.
stated.EUCF_relationship: EUCF is funded by the LIFE Programme and supports municipalities and local authorities to develop investment concepts for sustainable-energy investments.

funding_records.name: European City Facility (EUCF) - Call 7
interventions: Feasibility, engineering, legal, social, market, financial and energy-audit analyses
funding_records.finance_route: EUCF grant support for investment-concept development
funding_records.region_scope: EU-27 Member States, Iceland and Ukraine
funding_records.summary: Call 7 offered 71 fixed EUR 60,000 grants to develop investment concepts for sustainable-energy projects. It did not finance the underlying investments directly; grants could fund preparatory work required to develop the concepts. The application deadline was 18 December 2024 at 17:00 Brussels time.

funding_records.name: Smart-controlled community-integrated photovoltaic-plus-storage and electric mobility infrastructure for municipality-owned sites
funding_records.category: Renewable energy and sustainable urban mobility
interventions: Develop an investment concept for smart-controlled, community-integrated photovoltaic and storage infrastructure at municipality-owned sites
funding_records.finance_route: EUCF grant support for investment-concept development
funding_records.summary: Nicosia appears in EUCF's official Call 7 beneficiary list with this investment-concept title. EUCF announced in July 2025 that the 71 selected Call 7 beneficiaries would each receive its EUR 60,000 support package to develop clean-energy or energy-efficiency investment concepts and join capacity building.

funder_templates.template_name: EUCF Call 7 online application form described in the Guidelines for Applicants
funder_templates.output_format: Online form in the EUCF website user zone, completed in English, with mandatory supporting documents uploaded as PDF files

chapter_schema.title: Identification of the applicant
chapter_schema.description: Applicant identity, contacts, administrative codes, applicant type and population.

chapter_schema.title: Political commitments
chapter_schema.description: Climate or energy plan details, mitigation targets, political support and prior-beneficiary confirmation.

chapter_schema.title: Proposed investment project
chapter_schema.description: The project for which the applicant will develop the investment concept, including delivery, costs and impacts.

chapter_schema.title: Use of EUCF support
chapter_schema.description: Activities requiring the grant and whether the application was prepared internally or by an external expert.

required_fields: Applicant contact details and contact persons
Applicant type
LAU and NUTS 3 codes, or the applicable Ukrainian territorial codes
Lead applicant and participating-authority codes for groupings
Tax identification number or equivalent for an aggregating local public entity
Total population as an absolute number
SECAP or other local climate or energy plan upload
Plan title, covered period and description
At least one mitigation target with value, unit, target year and source-page number
Political-support letter signed by the Mayor or another relevant political representative using the mandatory EUCF template
Confirmation that the applicant has not previously benefited from EUCF support
Proposed investment-concept title
Proposed project description, local relevance and objectives
Foreseen project implementation period in months
Main investment sector
Governance structure, roles, decision-making, internal capacity and external-expert needs
Stakeholder-engagement strategy, activities, communication tools, timing and objectives
Intended measures with sector, CAPEX and expected energy, renewable-energy and greenhouse-gas impacts
Total expected sustainable-energy investment size in EUR
Potential for replication or upscaling
Activities for which EUCF support is needed and whether preparation is internal or external

funder_criteria.label: Eligible applicant type and location
funder_criteria.requirement_text: Applicants must be municipalities/local authorities, groupings of municipalities/local authorities, or local public entities aggregating municipalities/local authorities, located entirely in the EU-27, Iceland or Ukraine.
funder_criteria.normalized_rule: Applicant must be an eligible local-authority body wholly located in an eligible country.

funder_criteria.label: Climate or energy plan
funder_criteria.requirement_text: Applicant must have and submit a SECAP or another local climate and/or energy plan with mitigation targets.
funder_criteria.normalized_rule: Submit a qualifying local climate/energy plan containing at least one mitigation target.

funder_criteria.label: Political commitment
funder_criteria.requirement_text: Applicant must submit a letter of support for development of the investment concept, signed by the Mayor or another relevant political representative, using the EUCF template.
funder_criteria.normalized_rule: Submit the mandatory-template political-support letter signed by an authorized political representative.

funder_criteria.label: Monitoring commitment
funder_criteria.requirement_text: Applicant must commit to the two-year monitoring period following final submission of the investment concept.
funder_criteria.normalized_rule: Commit to the required two-year post-submission monitoring period.

funder_criteria.label: Prior EUCF beneficiary exclusion
funder_criteria.requirement_text: Beneficiaries from previous EUCF calls cannot participate again.
funder_criteria.normalized_rule: Applicant must not previously have received EUCF support.

funder_criteria.label: Ambition of the proposed investment concept
funder_criteria.requirement_text: The proposal is assessed for investment-size and impact ambition, cost-effectiveness, energy-efficiency focus, and replication/upscaling potential. No quality threshold applies.
funder_criteria.normalized_rule: Score up to 7 points; no minimum threshold.

funder_criteria.label: Governance structure
funder_criteria.requirement_text: Governance roles, decision-making, responsible persons, internal capacity, external-expert needs, clarity and robustness are assessed. A quality threshold of 2.5 out of 5 applies.

funder_criteria.label: Stakeholder engagement strategy
funder_criteria.requirement_text: Relevant stakeholders, their needs, engagement activities and objectives, communication instruments, timing, clarity and appropriateness are assessed. A quality threshold of 2.5 out of 5 applies.

funder_criteria.label: Coherence and plausibility
funder_criteria.requirement_text: Completeness, coherence, plausibility and realism of expected investment size and impacts are assessed. A quality threshold of 1.5 out of 3 applies; identical or similar content to another application results in rejection.
funder_criteria.normalized_rule: Score at least 1.5 of 3 points and do not submit duplicate/similar content.

sources.title: LIFE - European Climate, Infrastructure and Environment Executive Agency
European City Facility announces 71 new beneficiaries | EUCF

evidence.source_location: Page heading and Featured Projects programme labels
evidence.quote_or_summary: CINEA identifies LIFE as the LIFE Programme and labels projects as Programme for the Environment and Climate Action (LIFE).
The LIFE page describes EUCF as a European initiative supporting municipalities and local authorities across Europe.
The page states that LIFE is protecting the environment and climate.
Call 7 is funded by LIFE; EUCF supports local authorities to develop investment concepts for sustainable-energy investment.
The Call 7 page classifies the call under Energy Efficiency and Clean Energy.

The grant can fund feasibility, engineering, legal, social, market and financial analyses, energy audits and other investment-concept tasks; EUCF also provides technical and capacity-building support.
EUCF provides financial support to develop investment concepts and mobilise sustainable-energy finance.
Successful applicants receive a EUR 60,000 lump sum in the form of a grant.
Eligible applicants must be located entirely in the EU-27, Iceland or Ukraine.
EUCF finances investment-concept development with a fixed grant of EUR 60,000.
EUCF finances investment-concept development with a fixed grant of EUR 60,000.
The fixed Call 7 grant is stated as 60 000 EUR.
The official page explicitly says the call is closed and gives the final deadline as 18 December 2024.

evidence.source_location: About the call; What can be financed; Timeline
evidence.quote_or_summary: Call 7 supported 71 beneficiaries; grants funded resources and services to develop investment concepts rather than actual investments.
Applicant type and wholly eligible-country location are among the five criteria to which an applicant must answer yes.
Applicants must have and submit a SECAP or other local climate/energy plan with mitigation targets.
A political representative's support letter is required and the EUCF-provided template must be used.
Eligibility includes commitment to a two-year monitoring period after final investment-concept submission.
Prior EUCF beneficiaries may not participate again.
Ambition is scored out of 7 points and has no quality threshold.
Governance structure is scored out of 5 points and has a threshold of 2.5/5.
Stakeholder engagement is scored out of 5 points and has a threshold of 2.5/5.
Coherence and plausibility is scored out of 3 points with a 1.5/3 threshold; duplicate content results in rejection.

evidence.source_location: Full list of new beneficiaries table, Nicosia row
evidence.quote_or_summary: The beneficiary table lists Nicosia, Cyprus and this investment-concept title.

evidence.source_location: Full list of new beneficiaries table, Nicosia row
evidence.quote_or_summary: The table identifies Nicosia as the beneficiary city.

evidence.source_location: Full list of new beneficiaries table, Nicosia row
evidence.quote_or_summary: The Nicosia beneficiary row is listed under Cyprus.

evidence.source_location: Full list of new beneficiaries table, Nicosia row
evidence.quote_or_summary: The published title specifies photovoltaic-plus-storage and electric-mobility infrastructure at municipality-owned sites.
EUCF states that the 71 selected Call 7 beneficiaries receive a EUR 60,000 support package; Nicosia is listed among them.
The beneficiary-selection announcement is dated 18 July 2025.
Nicosia is in the published full list of 71 new selected beneficiaries.
The announcement says selected cities use funding to develop clean-energy or energy-efficiency investment concepts and join capacity building.

The official Guidelines describe an English online form in the EUCF user zone with four sections: applicant identification, political commitments, proposed investment project and use of EUCF support. They also specify the field content and the mandatory plan and political-support-letter annexes. This record describes the form structure; it is not the separate political-support-letter template.

evidence.source_location: Full list of new beneficiaries table, Nicosia row
evidence.quote_or_summary: The official Call 7 beneficiary table names Nicosia as the selected city.

evidence.source_location: Full list of new beneficiaries table, Nicosia row
evidence.quote_or_summary: The published Nicosia project title explicitly combines photovoltaic generation and storage with electric-mobility infrastructure, supporting the renewable-energy and sustainable-urban-mobility category.

evidence.source_location: Announcement introduction, About the EUCF, and Nicosia beneficiary row
evidence.quote_or_summary: The official announcement lists Nicosia among the Call 7 beneficiaries and states that EUCF financial support helps local authorities prepare investment concepts; it does not finance the underlying infrastructure investment.

evidence.source_location: Announcement introduction and About the EUCF
evidence.quote_or_summary: EUCF states that selected Call 7 beneficiaries receive EUR 60,000 lump sums, establishing the award as a fixed lump-sum grant.
The official Call 7 allocation table places Cyprus in the Southern Europe region.

evidence.source_location: Announcement introduction and Nicosia beneficiary row
evidence.quote_or_summary: The official announcement states that each listed selected beneficiary receives a EUR 60,000 support package; Nicosia is one of those listed beneficiaries.

gaps.reason: The captured Call 7 sources do not establish a co-financing requirement or rate for the EUR 60,000 lump-sum grant.
The official source establishes 2025 selection, but not grant-agreement signature, investment-concept completion or validation, or implementation status.
No captured official source identifies financing secured for Nicosia's underlying photovoltaic, storage, or electric-mobility investment.
No captured official source publishes the total investment amount or pipeline value for Nicosia's proposed investment concept.
None of the captured official webpages or Call 7 guidelines states a reusable-content license.
